notes: No single OECD-wide public merger register exists, so this build focuses on official competition authorities with dependable public case registers.
United States coverage reflects public DOJ civil merger enforcement filings, not confidential HSR notifications.
European Commission coverage uses the Commission’s official merger case search and adds the EU’s supranational merger reviews alongside national OECD authority data.

cases.title: Energy Bay - certain assets required to operate embedded networks for Centuria Capital Group members
cases.sector: 2619 Other Electricity Generation; 2630 Electricity Distribution; 2640 On Selling Electricity and Electricity Market Operation; 3232 Electrical Services

codes.value: 2619 Other Electricity Generation; 2630 Electricity Distribution; 2640 On Selling Electricity and Electricity Market Operation; 3232 Electrical Services

acquirers: The Trustee for Energy Bay Kappa Fund ABN - 37 949 909 024
cases.description: Energy Bay Kappa Fund Pty Ltd as trustee for the Energy Bay Kappa Fund (Energy Bay) proposes to acquire from various subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group (the CCG Members): energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to operate existing embedded networks at 45 sites, energy infrastructure assets and the exclusive right to install and operate embedded networks at a further 194 sites, and first and last rights of refusal to acquire analogous rights in respect of any further sites of the kind described in the transaction documents provided as part of the application. Energy Bay is part of Energy Bay Group, which is a distributed energy infrastructure company operating embedded networks at large commercial and industrial sites owned by third parties in New South Wales (NSW), Australian Capital Territory (ACT), Western Australia (WA), Victoria (VIC) and Queensland (QLD). Energy Bay Group also owns Diamond Energy Pty Ltd, an electricity retailer that operates in all National Electricity Market jurisdictions. The CCG Members are subsidiaries of the Centuria Capital Group, which is comprised of Centuria Capital Limited and Centuria Funds Management Limited. The CCG Members invest in and manage properties for investment funds. As part of that primary business activity, in each of NSW, ACT, VIC, QLD, South Australia, and WA, they currently: operate embedded network infrastructure at some of their sites, engage in behind the meter generation or storage at their sites, and supply and sell electricity to their commercial tenants connected to their embedded networks. Unlike Energy Bay the CCG Members do not, and do not plan to, operate embedded network infrastructure owned by third parties and/or at properties owned/occupied by third parties on behalf of those third parties.

cases.description: Siemens Mobility Holding B.V. (Siemens Mobility Holding) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in MerMec S.p.A (MerMec) (the Acquisition). Siemens Mobility Holding is an investment vehicle and wholly owned subsidiary of Siemens Aktiengesellschaft (Siemens AG), the Germany-based ultimate parent of the Siemens group of companies (Siemens). Siemens Mobility Holding and Siemens Mobility GmbH management, a Siemens AG subsidiary, (together SMO) operate in Australia through their subsidiary, Siemens Mobility Pty Ltd (Siemens Mobility). In Australia, Siemens Mobility is active in the supply of rolling stock, signalling systems, rail electrification services, rolling stock diagnostics and related software. To a limited historical extent, Siemens Mobility has previously supplied rail infrastructure diagnostics. MerMec is an Italian company headquartered in Monopoli, Italy. MerMec operates in Australia through its subsidiary, MerMec Australia Pty Ltd, which supplies rail infrastructure diagnostics (including diagnostics vehicles and onboard diagnostics components), rolling stock diagnostics components, related software, and quality control components for industrial manufacturing environments.

cases.description: The proposed joint venture combines Analogic Corporation (Analogic) with the assets and entities that comprise Leidos, Inc. (Leidos)’s Security Enterprise Solutions and Industrial and General Automation businesses (Leidos SES). Analogic, controlled by affiliates of Altaris Partners LLC (Altaris) is focussed on the healthcare, industrial and aviation security sectors. It is active in the design, development, manufacturing and support of advanced imaging and detection solutions, as well as power and automation systems. Analogic is active in aviation security globally and supplies explosive detection systems, including components of checked baggage explosive detection systems, to original equipment manufacturers overseas, which may then be supplied onto Australia. Altaris is an investment firm focussed on acquiring and building companies in the healthcare sector. It has invested in more than 50 companies across a range of healthcare subsectors. Leidos is an industry and technology company serving government and commercial customers under the Intelligence & Digital, Health, Homeland and Defense groupings. Leidos SES is active in aviation security globally and supplies cabin and checked baggage explosive detection systems in Australia. The merged entity will be owned by affiliates of Altaris and Leidos post-acquisition.

cases.title: Motorola Solutions - D-Fend Solutions
cases.description: Motorola Solutions, Inc. (MSI), through its subsidiary Motorola Solutions Finance EMEA Limited (MSF EMEA), proposes to acquire all of the issued and outstanding shares of D-Fend Solutions AD Ltd. (D-Fend Solutions) (the Acquisition). Upon completion, D-Fend Solutions will become a wholly owned indirect subsidiary of MSI. MSI is a US‑domiciled company that supplies mission‑critical communications, video security and access control, and command centre software products and solutions for public safety, government (including defence), and enterprise customers. MSF EMEA is the holding entity for MSI's operating subsidiaries in the UK, EU, Israel, and Norway. D‑Fend Solutions is an Israel‑based technology company specialising in counter‑unmanned aircraft system (C‑UAS) technology. D‑Fend Solutions supplies devices that detect, identify and mitigate unauthorised drones, including through radio frequency, radar and optical detection technologies. Its products are supplied primarily to public safety and critical infrastructure customers.

cases.title: VSA - SprayLine
cases.description: VSA Roads Pty Ltd (VSA) proposes to acquire the assets of SprayLine Road Services (SprayLine), a business operated by Head, Transport for Victoria (Head TfV) (the Acquisition). Head TfV is a statutory authority that operates and maintains the road system in Victoria. VSA supplies quarried and bituminous products, together with specialist contract paving and bituminous surfacing services (including spray sealing and line marking services) across South Australia, Victoria, New South Wales, and the Northern Territory. SprayLine supplies bituminous spray sealing, line marking, and road maintenance services across Victoria, New South Wales, and South Australia. The Victorian Government has decided to no longer operate an internal road maintenance provider, which has resulted in the divestment of SprayLine's spray sealing and line marking business. The Acquisition relates only to the spray sealing and line marking activities of SprayLine.

cases.title: Allianz – certain assets of nib’s Australian travel insurance business
cases.description: AWP Australia Pty Ltd (AWP), trading as Allianz Global Assistance, proposes to acquire certain assets of the travel insurance distribution portfolio of nib Travel Pty Limited (nib) (the Acquisition). AWP is part of the Allianz Group (Allianz), a global insurance provider whose holding company, Allianz SE, is based in Germany. AWP issues travel insurance products underwritten by Allianz Australia Insurance Limited. nib is part of the nib Group, a general insurance provider operating in Australia and New Zealand. nib Group primarily supplies health insurance. It also manages and distributes travel insurance policies in Australia under different brands, which are underwritten by Pacific International Insurance. AWP and a nib Group entity also propose to enter into a distribution agreement as part of the transaction. This agreement will enable nib Group to continue distributing white-labelled Allianz travel insurance products to its health insurance customers as an authorised representative of AWP.

cases.title: Partnered Health - Ryan Plaza Medical Services
acquirers: PH MEDICAL CENTRES PTY LTD ABN - 84 615 268 043
cases.description: PH Medical Centres Pty Ltd (Partnered Health) proposes to acquire Ryan Plaza Medical Services Pty Ltd (Ryan Plaza) (the Acquisition). Partnered Health is part of the Partnered Health Group and an indirectly wholly-owned subsidiary of Partnered Health Pty Ltd (PHPL). The Partnered Health Group comprises two business divisions: the PH Primary Care Group and the Corporate Health and Wellbeing Group. PHPL is part of a larger group of companies which are majority owned by Quadrant Private Equity (QPE) through Quadrant Private Equity No. 6 (QPE Fund 6). Partnered Health together with other PHPL subsidiaries (PH Primary Care Group) provide primary care services through 67 general practice clinics and medical centres across Australia, engaging approximately 570 general practitioners. The PH Primary Care Group also operates skin cancer clinics, allied health providers (physiotherapy and dietetics) and TeleWell, a 24/7 telehealth platform. The Partnered Health Group’s Corporate Health and Wellbeing Group provides corporate and occupational healthcare services and solutions, including occupational health services, psychology and mental health services, nutritional services, onsite medical and emergency response and training solutions and services provided by physiotherapists, massage therapists, exercise physiologists, and dietitians Ryan Plaza operates a single medical centre called “Medicine on Second” at Maroochydore, Queensland. Ryan Plaza provides primary care services through 11 general practitioners.

cases.sector: 0142 Beef Cattle Farming (Specialised); 0143 Beef Cattle Feedlots (Specialised); 1111 Meat Processing

codes.value: 0142 Beef Cattle Farming (Specialised); 0143 Beef Cattle Feedlots (Specialised); 1111 Meat Processing

acquirers: SAPPHIRE CATTLE COMPANY PTY LTD ABN - 14 600 389 286
Dawson Land Investments Company Pty Ltd as trustee for the Dawson Land Trust ACN - 600 506 778
cases.description: Sapphire Cattle Company, an entity within the Hewitt group (Hewitt) is proposing to acquire Nolan Meats Pty Ltd (Nolan Meats), including land and assets, via connected entities (the Acquisition). Hewitt is a majority-owned subsidiary of the Public Sector Pension Investment Board (PSP Investments), an investment management fund established by the Parliament of Canada. The remaining shares in Hewitt are owned by the Hewitt family. Hewitt is a red meat producer, headquartered in Brisbane, which farms cattle and sheep, undertakes secondary processing of beef into value-added products (such as beef patties), and distributes (including the packaging, marketing and sale of) beef to supermarkets, the food service industry, butchers and international customers. Hewitt predominantly produces organic beef products. Nolan Meats is a beef producer headquartered in Gympie, Queensland, which undertakes lot feeding of cattle, primary processing of cattle, and the wholesale distribution (including packaging, marketing and sale) of beef to supermarkets, the food service industry, butchers and international customers. Nolan Meats’ core assets include a primary processing facility with an annual capacity of 260,000 head of cattle, located in Gympie, and a 4,000 standard cattle unit-licensed beef cattle feedlot situated 65 kilometres west of the processing facility.

cases.title: Journey Beyond - Kelsian Tourism Portfolio
cases.sector: 4400 Accommodation; 4820 Water Passenger Transport; 5010 Scenic and Sightseeing Transport; 7220 Travel Agency and Tour Arrangement Services; 9139 Amusement and Other Recreational Activities n.e.c.

codes.value: 4400 Accommodation; 4820 Water Passenger Transport; 5010 Scenic and Sightseeing Transport; 7220 Travel Agency and Tour Arrangement Services; 9139 Amusement and Other Recreational Activities n.e.c.

acquirers: EXPERIENCE AUSTRALIA GROUP PTY LTD ABN - 56 614 713 003
cases.description: Experience Australia Group Pty Ltd (EAG) proposes to acquire, by way of share and asset purchases, a portfolio of tourism assets (Tourism Portfolio) from Kelsian Group Limited or its subsidiaries or controlled entities (together, Kelsian) (the Acquisition). EAG operates a range of tourism businesses across Australia under the brand name “Journey Beyond”. EAG’s tourism businesses include tours, accommodation, rail journeys, scenic flights, and marine sightseeing. EAG’s subsidiary, Outback Spirit Tours Pty Ltd (Outback Spirit), supplies touring packages to remote destinations across Western Australia, the Northern Territory, Queensland and South Australia. Kelsian is an ASX-listed global operator of bus, motorcoach, tourism and marine services. In Australia, it supplies public transport services across six states and territories through Transit Systems, and marine transport services (including commuter and leisure ferries) through SeaLink Marine & Tourism. In April 2025, Kelsian announced an intention to divest its Australian tourism assets, known as the Tourism Portfolio. In February 2026, Kelsian announced it had entered into binding agreements for the sale of the companies and businesses forming its Tourism Portfolio to EAG. Kelsian’s Tourism Portfolio comprises: Adelaide Sightseeing Tours: tour services in the Adelaide region Murray Princess Cruise: cruise experiences along the Murray River Tasmania Ferries: ferry services between Bruny Island and Tasmania mainland Sydney Harbour Cruises and Ferries: harbour cruise and ferry services in Sydney K'gari Resorts, Tours and Ferries: resort accommodation, tour and ferry services on K'gari (Fraser Island) Northern Territory Ferries: ferry and boat services in the Northern Territory Swan River Cruises: cruise and charter services operating from the Barrack Street Jetty in Perth Swan Valley Tours: river cruise and coach tour services from Perth to the Swan Valley Bells Functions: waterfront function and event space at the Barrack Street Jetty in Perth In addition, the Tourism Portfolio includes SeaLink Rottnest, the passenger ferry service from Perth and Fremantle to Rottnest Island. In relation to this Acquisition, Journey Beyond and Kelsian overlap in the supply of tourism experiences in Sydney, the Whitsundays, K'gari and South Australia. Outback Spirit currently purchases accommodation and ferry services from Kelsian in K'gari and Bruny Island. ACCC is also assessing EAG’s proposed acquisition of SeaLink Rottnest.

Symal Group is a civil construction and engineering services provider across the roads, rail, defence, ports and energy sectors, including demolition, bulk earthworks, site preparation, haulage and tip operations services. Symal Group operates businesses under the Symal, Sycle, Unyte, Searo and Wamarra brands. cases.sector: 6970 Veterinary Services; 9539 Other Personal Services n.e.c.

codes.value: 6970 Veterinary Services; 9539 Other Personal Services n.e.c.

cases.description: VC Operations (WA) Pty Ltd, which is part of the Vets Central group of companies (Vets Central), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets of Hills Veterinary Centre from veterinarian, Jonathan Julian Carruthers (the Acquisition). Vets Central is an Australian owned veterinary group that owns and operates 78 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia, including Familypet Vet Coromandel Valley, which is located approximately 3.5 km from Hills Veterinary Centre. Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services). Hills Veterinary Centre is a full-service veterinary practice in Blackwood, South Australia. The business provides a range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for household pets (extending to exotic pets) in the local area.

cases.title: KINETIC - DYSONS BUS SERVICES
cases.sector: 4621 Interurban and Rural Bus Transport; 4622 Urban Bus Transport (Including Tramway)

codes.value: 4621 Interurban and Rural Bus Transport; 4622 Urban Bus Transport (Including Tramway)

acquirers: KINETIC HOLDING COMPANY PTY LTD ABN - 49 601 254 880
cases.description: Kinetic Holding Company Pty Ltd (Kinetic) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in L.C. Dyson’s Bus Services Pty Ltd (Dysons, together with its affiliates, the Dysons Group) pursuant to a share sale agreement. Kinetic also proposes to acquire certain freehold and leasehold interests in bus depot assets currently leased by the Dysons Group and owned by related parties of Dysons’ shareholders, namely 3D Management Properties Pty Ltd, 324 Gen Investments Pty Ltd, 3D Properties (Kyneton) Pty Ltd and 3D Properties (Wodonga) Pty Ltd. Kinetic is proposing to acquire the bus depot assets under separate Land Sale Contracts. The share sale agreement and each of the Land Sale Contracts are interdependent, with simultaneous settlement and completion. Given the interdependencies between each of these agreements, Kinetic is notifying the proposed acquisitions together as a single notification for the purposes of s 51ABX(5) of the CCA (Proposed Transaction). Kinetic is a transportation company that operates bus services in Australia and overseas. This is predominantly under the Kinetic brand but also (in Australia) in: • the Northern Territory, Queensland and Western Australia under the Kinetic Specialised Resources brand; • in Victoria, Sydney and Western Australia under the SkyBus brand; and • in Queensland under the Koala Koaches brand and SkyBus brands. These services include, depending on the region, urban route and school services, regional bus services, and charter bus services, airport transfers bus services and some specialised bus services, for example to the mining and resources sector. Dysons is a family-owned coach and bus operator based in Melbourne. It provides services in Victoria, regional New South Wales and regional South Australia under the Dysons brand. These services include urban route services, school services, regional bus services, rail replacement services and charter services.

cases.title: SLB – S&P Global’s Geoscience Software Portfolio
cases.sector: 1090 Other Mining Support Services; 5921 Data Processing and Web Hosting Services; 6923 Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services; 6925 Scientific Testing and Analysis Services; 7000 Computer System Design and Related Services

codes.value: 1090 Other Mining Support Services; 5921 Data Processing and Web Hosting Services; 6923 Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services; 6925 Scientific Testing and Analysis Services; 7000 Computer System Design and Related Services

cases.description: SLB N.V. (together with its subsidiaries, SLB) is proposing to acquire S&P Global Inc.’s (S&P Global) geoscience software portfolio (the Acquisition). SLB is a global technology company that provides equipment, software and technical services, including subsurface evaluation, drilling and production services, to customers in the energy sector. Relevantly, SLB supplies exploration and production software including Petrel, Eclipse, Intersect, OFM, Techlog, Omega, Petromod, DrillPlan, Pipesim and Olga. S&P Global is an information, data, analytics, ratings, indices and commodity-information business. S&P Global also operates an energy division which provides independent information and benchmark prices for the energy and commodity markets; and geoscience software used to scope, plan and monitor oil and gas well development. S&P Global’s geoscience portfolio (the target of the Acquisition) comprises the following software, used in upstream oil and gas exploration and production workflows: Kingdom, Petra, Harmony Enterprise, Analytics Explorer, SubPUMP, PowerTools, FieldDIRECT, Piper, WellTest and Element.

cases.sector: 7000 Computer System Design and Related Services

codes.value: 7000 Computer System Design and Related Services

acquirers: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. NASDAQ - SPCX
cases.description: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX), through its subsidiary, proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Anysphere, Inc. (Anysphere). SpaceX is the ultimate parent company of the SpaceX group, which includes X.AI Corp (xAI), an artificial intelligence (AI) company, and X Corp., which owns and operates the X social media platform (formerly Twitter). xAI develops the Grok family of large language models and offers Grok Build, a terminal-native AI coding tool, to Grok and X subscribers. Grok Build currently runs on xAI’s Grok model family. Anysphere develops and operates Cursor, an AI coding tool used by software developers to write and review code with the assistance of AI models. Cursor users can select from a range of third-party large language models (including Grok models) or Anysphere’s proprietary Composer model to power the platform’s AI assistant features. Composer is purpose-built for Cursor and is not offered as a standalone general-purpose model for other purposes.

cases.title: Amazon – Globalstar
cases.description: Amazon.com, Inc. (Amazon) proposes to acquire Globalstar, Inc (Globalstar), its subsidiaries, assets and facilities. Amazon supplies retail services, media content, electronic devices and on-demand technology services (including cloud computing). Amazon also operates Amazon Leo, which will supply high-capacity broadband/fixed satellite service (FSS) services to government agencies, business and consumers using low Earth orbit satellites. Amazon Leo plans to launch its commercial service in mid-2026. In Australia, Amazon Leo has an agreement with NBN Co to deliver fixed-satellite broadband services to regional, remote and rural Australian customers. Globalstar supplies global narrowband/mobile satellite service (MSS) connectivity services for voice and data communications applications. Globalstar provides these services to government agencies, businesses and consumers, using allocated MSS spectrum. Globalstar currently provides Apple with direct-to-device (D2D) satellite connectivity services on certain iPhone and Apple Watch models, allowing users to send and receive messages while outside of cellular and Wi-Fi coverage (e.g. to text emergency services or receive roadside assistance). Amazon has signed an agreement to continue providing Apple with D2D services post-acquisition.

cases.title: Hapag-Lloyd - ZIM
acquirers: Hapag-Lloyd Aktiengesellschaft German company registration number - HRB97937
cases.description: Hapag-Lloyd Aktiengesellschaft (HLAG) proposes to acquire 100% of the outstanding shares and voting rights of ZIM Integrated Shipping Services Ltd (ZIM). The proposed acquisition will be effected by a newly-formed and wholly owned Israeli subsidiary of HLAG, Norazia (Israel) Ltd, merging with and into ZIM. HLAG is an international container liner shipping company, offering global transport services for container cargo and has the fifth largest TEU in the world. Hapag-Lloyd is active across all major east-west and north-south trades, though it submits it maintains a focus in the Transatlantic, Middle East, Latin America, and Intra-America trades. ZIM is a public company listed on the New York Stock Exchange in the USA (NYSE: ZIM). Its headquarters are in Israel. Globally, ZIM is active in container line shipping and related services, including logistics services (such as freight forwarding). In Australia, HLAG and ZIM overlap in supply of container liner shipping services between Asia and Australia. Specifically, the Parties overlap in relation to trades between the Australian East Coast and Busan (South Korea), Qingdao (China), Shanghai (China), Ningbo (China) and Yantian (China). ZIM also has a limited presence in the downstream freight forwarding services market in Australia. HLAG does not conduct these activities in Australia.

cases.description: OpenAI Group PBC, an entity controlled by the OpenAI Foundation (OpenAI), proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital of Gitpod, Inc. (known as Ona). OpenAI develops artificial intelligence (AI) foundational models, including its GPT family of models, as well as foundation model-based services such as ChatGPT. OpenAI also supplies Codex, an AI-powered agent designed to enable software developers to perform and automate various software engineering tasks, subject to human direction and review. Codex is typically accessed through user-facing interfaces and developer tools and is powered by OpenAI’s own foundation models. Ona (previously known as Gitpod) supplies a platform that enables enterprise customers to deploy and orchestrate AI-powered agents to run software engineering tasks within the customer’s own infrastructure or cloud environment. These agents can run autonomously in the background and at scale, including when individual developers are not actively using their laptops. Ona’s platform works across multiple cloud platforms and with multiple foundation models.

cases.description: WSP Australia Pty Limited (WSP) proposes to acquire substantially all of the assets and the business of Geotechnical Testing and Interpretation Pty Ltd (GTI) as a going concern. WSP is a subsidiary of the WSP group, a global engineering consulting services firm. As part of its service offering in Australia, WSP supplies a range of geotechnical testing and interpretation services (geotechnical testing services) to customers for onshore projects. Geotechnical testing services involve testing samples from various sources and environments and interpreting the test results to support geotechnical engineering analysis and related work. WSP operates five accredited geotechnical testing laboratories in Australia (located in Melbourne, Brisbane, Perth, Adelaide and Olympic Dam). GTI supplies a range of geotechnical testing services in Australia to customers for both onshore and offshore projects, with a focus on providing offshore marine testing services. GTI operates one geotechnical testing laboratory in Australia (located in Perth).

(INNIO Jenbacher Netherlands) (together with their affiliates, the INNIO Group) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Enerflex Service Pty Limited (Enerflex). The Target Companies provide aftermarket services (spare parts, repair and maintenance) for gas compression and energy infrastructure. (the Acquisition). INNIO Group is a global manufacturer of gas power units under the Jenbacher and Waukesha brands. INNIO Group manufactures gas power units and genuine spare parts at its facilities (primarily in Austria for Jenbacher and the United States, and Canada for Waukesha) and supplies these globally through distributors, including in Australia. Enerflex Australia is an aftermarket services provider for energy infrastructure operators in Australia. It provides maintenance, repair, replacement of spare parts, field services, retrofits, and the supply of gas power units (new and refurbished as replacements) as part of their aftermarket services for a wide variety of gas compression and power generation units and other related equipment, such as compressor units and electric motors.

Sika AG is a globally active speciality chemicals group headquartered in Switzerland. Sika AG supplies chemical-based admixtures, mortars, sealants and adhesives, damping and reinforcing materials, structural strengthening systems, industrial flooring, as well as roofing and waterproofing systems. Their products are used in the building sector and by manufacturing industries. Akkim, founded in Turkey, is a chemical product manufacturer specialising in the manufacture of sealants, adhesives, PU foams, waterproofing products and aerosols. cases.description: Akzo Nobel N.V. (AkzoNobel) and Axalta Coating Systems Ltd. AkzoNobel will be renamed, with the final name to be determined at a later stage. AkzoNobel is a global paints and coatings manufacturer headquartered in Amsterdam, the Netherlands. AkzoNobel produces and markets paints and coatings, including automotive and speciality coatings, powder coatings, industrial coatings, and marine and protective coatings, for professional, do-it-yourself, and industrial end users. Axalta is a global coatings manufacturer and distributer headquartered in Philadelphia, USA. Axalta supplies a range of coating solutions, including vehicle refinish coatings for light vehicles, commercial vehicles, electric motors, and building facades. AkzoNobel and Axalta overlap in the supply of vehicle refinish and powder coatings in Australia.

cases.description: Little Company of Mary Health Care Ltd (Calvary) proposes to acquire the business and assets of Hobart Private Hospital (Hobart Private) from Healthscope pursuant to a Business and Asset Sale Agreement (the Acquisition). Calvary is a not-for-profit operator of public and private hospitals in Australia, present in New South Wales, South Australia, Victoria and the Australian Capital Territory. Calvary currently operates four private hospitals in Tasmania: St Vincent's Hospital (Launceston); St Luke's Hospital (Launceston); Lenah Valley Hospital (Lenah Valley, Hobart); and St John's Hospital (South Hobart). Hobart Private is a private hospital co-located with and adjacent to Hobart's main public hospital, Royal Hobart Hospital, operated by the Tasmanian Department of Health. Hobart Private offers healthcare services including medical, surgical and emergency care. Hobart Private is currently operated by Healthscope, a private hospital operator with 36 hospitals Australia wide. In May 2025, Healthscope’s parent entities went into voluntary administration, and receivers and administrators were appointed by its lenders and the Healthscope Board, respectively, to conduct an orderly sale of the hospitals in its network.

cases.description: Transocean Limited (Transocean) proposes to acquire Valaris Limited (Valaris) (the Acquisition) by way of merger, where Transocean shareholders will own approximately 53% of the combined entity and Valaris shareholders will own the remaining 47%. Transocean and Valaris both provide offshore contract drilling services globally through a fleet of owned and operated mobile offshore drilling units including jackups, semisubmersibles and drillships (together, rigs). Transocean and Valaris supply these rigs, associated equipment and work crews to oil and gas producing customers for use in offshore exploration, appraisal, development drilling, workovers and ‘plug and abandonment’ activities. Transocean owns and operates a rig fleet comprised of 20 drillships (three of which are stacked) and seven semisubmersibles. Stacked rigs refer to rigs that are not currently in service and are instead in a preservation or dormant state. Transocean does not own or operate any jackups. In Australia, Transocean currently has two semisubmersibles under contract and a drillship due to commence operation in Australia in 2027. Valaris currently owns and operates 31 jackups (seven of which are currently stacked), one semisubmersible and 13 drillships (three of which are currently stacked). In Australia, Valaris currently has one jackup under contract.

cases.title: Uber - Blacklane
cases.description: Uber Technologies, Inc (Uber) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Blacklane GmbH (Blacklane) by way of a share purchase and transfer agreement (the Acquisition). Following completion, Blacklane will become a wholly owned indirect subsidiary of Uber. Uber is a US-based ride-hailing and food delivery platform founded in 2009 that operates in over 70 countries, including Australia, where it launched in 2012. Uber offers a range of products on its ride-hailing platform with different levels of service that operate largely on an on-demand model. These products include its core products, Uber X, Uber X Share, Uber XL and Uber Comfort, as well as its premium product Uber Black, which offers higher-end vehicles along with higher-rated and more experienced drivers. Blacklane is a German-headquartered global luxury chauffeur platform, founded in 2011 and which operates in over 50 countries. The majority of its services are provided in the United States, United Kingdom and Middle East. Blacklane’s platform connects customers with licensed local chauffeurs offering a high level of service, described as “white glove”. In Australia, Blacklane’s services are generally booked at least an hour in advance via Blacklane’s platform, accessed using an app or the web. Blacklane’s services are aimed at senior corporate executives and high net-worth individuals. Blacklane does not own or operate a fleet of vehicles, and instead operates through non-exclusive contracts with local service providers who manage their fleet and drivers and facilitate the journey.

cases.description: CVS Vets (Australia) Proprietary Limited, which is part of CVS Group Plc (together, CVS Group), proposes to acquire 60% of the shares in Newman Vet Pty Ltd (trading as Paws at Prospect Vet and Eyes at Prospect Vet) (Paws), a small animal veterinary practice in Adelaide (the Acquisition). The existing owners of Paws will retain the remaining 40% of shares in Paws. CVS Group is an integrated veterinary services provider based in the United Kingdom and listed on the London Stock Exchange. In Australia, CVS Group owns a number of small animal veterinary practices in Queensland, New South Wales, the Australian Capital Territory, Victoria, South Australia and Western Australia. CVS Group currently owns three veterinary practices in Adelaide: Walkerville Vet, Pet Universe Manningham and Pet Universe Northgate. CVS Group and Paws both supply small animal veterinary services in Adelaide. Small animal veterinary practices attend to small animals (such as cats, dogs, guinea pigs, birds, rabbits and other pets), rather than larger animals (such as cattle, sheep and horses), and offer services such as consultation, general and preventative health care/advice, surgery, microchipping, dentistry, radiology, pathology, pharmacy and related services such as puppy school. In addition to first opinion small animal vet services, Paws also provides specialist ophthalmic vet services on referral from first opinion vets. CVS does not own any specialist vet services clinics in South Australia.

HMAH, formerly known as Hanson Holdings Australia Pty Ltd, together with its related entities in Australia (collectively, HMA) supplies a range of construction materials, including RMX, quarry products (including coarse and fine aggregates), asphalt and sustainable / recycled construction materials. HMA and MAAS (the Parties) overlap in the supply of the following products in the following regions: RMX in Greater Melbourne and Geelong, Central Queensland (Rockhampton and Blackwater), NSW (Tamworth, Dubbo, Illawarra and Shoalhaven), coarse aggregates in Greater Melbourne, Central Queensland (Biloela), South East Queensland (to the extent that MAAS is the landholder of the Yatala quarry, which is operated by Crushing Dynamics under a lease), NSW (Dubbo, Illawarra and Shoalhaven), fine aggregates in Greater Melbourne, asphalt in Greater Melbourne (in the future once MAAS’s plant in Pakenham becomes operational), South East Queensland, and heavy C&D waste disposal and recycling services in Greater Melbourne. The Parties’ coarse aggregates and fine aggregates products are upstream inputs into RMX and asphalt, and can also be used for other end uses such as road base, ballast, fill material, drainage and landscaping.

cases.sector: 0529 Other Agriculture and Fishing Support Services; 1842 Veterinary Pharmaceutical and Medicinal Product Manufacturing

codes.value: 0529 Other Agriculture and Fishing Support Services; 1842 Veterinary Pharmaceutical and Medicinal Product Manufacturing

cases.description: Zoetis Holdings LLC (Zoetis), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Zoetis Inc., proposes to acquire the global animal genomic testing business (Neogen Genomics) of Neogen Corporation (the Acquisition). Animal genomic testing involves the analysis of an animal’s DNA from samples like hair or tissue and integrating this genetic information with performance and pedigree data primarily maintained by breed societies and industry associations. This process increases the accuracy in prediction of the animal’s genetic merit, helping breeders (e.g. farmers) make early, informed decisions in relation to traits like health, fertility, production (milk/meat) and feed efficiency, as well as physical characteristics. Both Zoetis and Neogen Corporation are US-listed companies that supply genomic testing services in respect of livestock globally, including in Australia. Zoetis supplies genomic testing services from its laboratory located in Kalamazoo, Michigan in the US, and Neogen Genomics supplies services from its laboratory located in Brisbane. The Acquisition will involve Zoetis acquiring all of the shares in the companies within the Neogen Corporation group that supply genomic testing services globally, as well as certain non-Australian assets of Neogen Genomics.

cases.description: Bertelsmann SE & Co. KGaA (Bertelsmann) proposes to merge its music publishing and recording business, Bertelsmann Music Group (BMG), with the music publishing and recording business operated under the Concord label and trademark (Concord), which is currently majority owned and controlled by Axton Holdings, LLC (Axton). The proposed merger transaction will involve Bertelsmann and Axton incorporating a new holding company (New MusicCo), to which both Bertelsmann – via subsidiaries comprising BMG – and Axton via Concord will contribute their respective music recording and publishing businesses (the Merger). In Australia, BMG acquires rights from songwriters and composers and supplies music publishing services and recorded music products. Concord is an independent, US-based music publishing and recording company supporting artists globally. In Australia, Concord supplies music publishing services and recorded music products.

Complete Air Supply supplies HVAC units, and manufactures and supplies HVAC ductware and fittings, to installers for domestic residence installs or commercial air conditioning projects. cases.description: Service Stream Limited (Service Stream), via a wholly owned subsidiary, proposes to acquire all of the issued share capital in RiE Group Pty Ltd (RiE). Service Stream’s business is divided into three operating divisions, which are Telecommunications, Transport and Utilities. Service Stream’s Utilities division, which is relevant to the proposed acquisition, provides services to the water, power and industrial sectors, including: design, civil, structural, electrical, mechanical, control engineering and metering services to the water industry operations, maintenance, design and installation services in relation to gas assets distribution and metering services for electricity networks including network maintenance, asset inspection and installation services. RiE is a privately-owned industrial electrical and instrumentation provider established in 2008, based in Queensland’s Western Downs region. RiE offers specialised electrical and instrumentation infrastructure services, equipment rental and labour hire solutions targeted to industrial clients who rely on electrical infrastructure to operate their plant or facilities, such as power generation, renewable energy, oil and gas, and utilities.

cases.title: ANZ Banking Group - Worldline Australia
cases.sector: 6419 Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

codes.value: 6419 Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

acquirers: AUSTRALIA AND NEW ZEALAND BANKING GROUP LIMITED ABN - 11 005 357 522
cases.description: Australia and New Zealand Banking Group Limited (ANZ), an entity within a group of companies ultimately held by ANZ Group Holdings Limited (ANZ group), proposes to acquire Worldline SA (WL)’s interest in Worldline Australia Pty Ltd, trading as ANZ Worldline Payment Solutions (ANZWL), under a share sale and purchase agreement. ANZWL is currently a joint venture between ANZ and WL (the Parties), with ANZ group holding 49% and WL holding 51% of the share capital of ANZWL. Following the acquisition, ANZ group will wholly own ANZWL. ANZ Group Holdings Limited is an ASX-listed financial services company. ANZ provides a range of banking products and services to retail and business customers in Australia and New Zealand, and to institutional customers globally. WL is a global merchant acquiring business, headquartered in France, and Europe’s largest merchant acquiring services provider. WL provides payment acceptance services and products to business customers internationally, including mobile authentication, digital banking, mobile payments and wallets, omni-commerce services, and account-to-account payments. ANZWL was established in April 2022 when ANZ divested its merchant acquiring services business and referred business customers who required merchant acquiring services to ANZWL. The joint venture was intended to deliver enhanced payment solutions to ANZ’s merchant customers, while allowing ANZ group to retain a significant ownership interest and an ongoing commercial relationship with the business. The Parties overlap in the supply of payment solutions, including merchant acquiring, acceptance and value-added services, to business customers in Australia.

cases.title: ANZ, CBA, NAB and Westpac – shares in joint venture and interests in Joint Cash Pool
acquirers: AUSTRALIA AND NEW ZEALAND BANKING GROUP LIMITED ABN - 11 005 357 522
NATIONAL AUSTRALIA INVESTMENT CAPITAL PTY LTD ABN - 27 073 242 010
cases.description: ANZ, CBA, NAB and Westpac (together, the Major Banks) propose to enter into a joint venture for the establishment of a new joint single cash pool (Joint Cash Pool) between the Major Banks with respect to wholesale and ‘commercial cash’ distribution (Proposed JV). Currently, the Major Banks purchase banknotes from the RBA and coins from the Mint directly and each Major Bank has its own cash reserves, which are managed as four separate ‘virtual’ cash pools. Each Major Bank uses its own cash pool to support the supply of cash to its own branches and ATMs, and on to customers. In order to establish the Proposed JV, the Major Banks’ existing cash holdings would be amalgamated into the Joint Cash Pool and the Major Banks would each acquire a 25% shareholding in an incorporated operating entity (JVCo) and a 25% interest in the Joint Cash Pool (together, the Acquisitions). JVCo’s operations and the Joint Cash Pool will form part of the broader cash distribution system, following Westpac’s decision to cease its current arrangements as the sole supplier of commercial cash. Conduct related to the establishment and operation of JVCo is also the subject of a separate application of authorisation (AA1000729) under section 88 of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (CCA). Once JVCo is established, and subject to the separate ACCC authorisation application, it is proposed that JVCo: will hold the cash in the Joint Cash Pool as bailee for the Major Banks as joint bailors; would supply cash from the Joint Cash Pool to each of the Major Banks (in their capacity as customers of JVCo), in order to meet their cash requirements for their own branches and ATMs, and to in-scope commercial cash distributors for supply to their business cash customers such as major retailers, small businesses and smaller banks; and would become the sole supplier of commercial cash from the Joint Cash Pool.

cases.sector: 4610 Road Freight Transport; 4710 Rail Freight Transport; 5211 Stevedoring Services; 5212 Port and Water Transport Terminal Operations; 5292 Freight Forwarding Services; 5299 Other Transport Support Services n.e.c.; 5301 Grain Storage Services; 5309 Other Warehousing and Storage Services

codes.value: 4610 Road Freight Transport; 4710 Rail Freight Transport; 5211 Stevedoring Services; 5212 Port and Water Transport Terminal Operations; 5292 Freight Forwarding Services; 5299 Other Transport Support Services n.e.c.; 5301 Grain Storage Services; 5309 Other Warehousing and Storage Services

cases.description: A consortium comprising funds managed by Macquarie Asset Management (MAM) and co-investors proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Qube Holdings Limited (Qube) by way of scheme of arrangement (Acquisition). The consortium, through a series of Special Purpose Vehicles (SPVs), is to invest in Rubik Australia Holdings Pty Limited (ACN 694 529 149) (TopCo) and a wholly owned subsidiary of TopCo, Rubik Australia Pty Limited (BidCo), has entered into the scheme implementation deed (SID) with Qube. The consortium is made up of three direct investors, UniSuper (20%), Pontegadea Shareholdings Luxembourg Sàrl (an entity indirectly owned by Pontegadea, holding 15% through a dedicated SPV), and the MAM Box (65%). The MAM Box is an investment vehicle that comprises a number of Macquarie managed funds and large superannuation, sovereign wealth, and global investment vehicles. MAM, through various MAM-managed funds, will have a controlling interest in the MAM Box, which will result in MAM having a controlling interest in Qube. Other investors that will hold interests in Qube through the MAM Box, as well as existing interests in logistics and infrastructure assets, include Temasek, GIC Private Limited (GIC) and CalPERS. MAM MAM is the asset management arm of Macquarie Group Limited. MAM manages investments by clients in significant infrastructure assets in Australia (including airports, telecommunication assets, data centres and electricity distributors). MAM provides investment management services to the trustee of The Infrastructure Fund (TIF). TIF holds a 50% interest in the Port of Newcastle. The other 50% stake is held by China Merchants Port Holdings Company Limited. Qube Qube is an integrated provider of import and export logistics services, operating more than 200 sites across Australia, New Zealand and Southeast Asia and employs ~10,000 people. Qube’s service offering is underpinned by a network of assets including ports, terminals, rail, road and storage infrastructure. Qube’s operations are organised into three divisions: Logistics & Infrastructure (L&I), Ports & Bulk (P&B) and a 50% interest in Patrick Terminals. Qube is a tenant and port user at the Port of Newcastle. It supplies a range of services at the Port in relation to bulk, breakbulk and general stevedoring services, rail haulage services and grain export terminal services (through Newcastle Agri Terminal). Other co-investors UniSuper UniSuper already holds ~15% of Qube and, with its additional equity contribution, the Acquisition will result in it increasing its stake by approximately 5%. UniSuper also currently holds an approximate 11% interest in TIF, giving it an indirect look-through interest in the Port of Newcastle of approximately 5.5%. It is also a shareholder in Gardior Pty Ltd (Gardior), the trustee appointed to act on behalf of TIF. Temasek Temasek is a global investment company headquartered in Singapore with a presence in 9 countries. Temasek will acquire a 12.5% look-through stake in Qube. Temasek’s wholly owned portfolio entity, PSA International Pte. Ltd. (PSA), owns BDP Asia Pacific (Australia) Pty Ltd, which is a freight forwarding supplier offering freight, transport and logistics services across Australia. PSA also has a 20% interest in Hutchison Port Holdings Limited, which through Hutchison Ports Australia operates container terminals at Port Botany and Port of Brisbane. Temasek currently has a 6.48% interest in Patrick Terminals. Temasek’s wholly owned entity, Heliconia Capital Management (Heliconia), manages a fund that holds a majority interest in Pacific International Lines (PIL). GIC GIC will acquire a 6.59% look-through stake in Qube. GIC through its affiliate, Buckland Investment Pte Ltd (Buckland), has a 12% interest in Pacific National, a rail freight services provider and an 11% interest in Linx Cargo Care, a transportation and logistics company. CalPERS Smaller co-investors in the MAM Box include CalPERS, a Californian pension fund, investing through a MAM Controlled Sidecar investment vehicle, which will have a look-through interest in Qube of 2.7%. CalPERS holds an equity stake in the Port of Melbourne of 9.95% which is held via a separate account managed by Queensland Investment Corporation (QIC). NPS The National Pension Service (NPS) is a state-owned public pension fund based in South Korea. NPS will acquire a 4.3% look-through stake in Qube via the MAM Box (to be acquired in two stages, initially 1.3% and a further 3.0% to be committed shortly thereafter, both to occur prior to completion). As a result, MAM’s look-through stake in Qube will reduce by 4.3%. NPS holds a 10% equity stake in the Port of Melbourne which is held via a Global Infrastructure Partners Australia feeder trust.
